purpose: Map the smallest repository surfaces needed to distinguish default open-or-activate behavior, explicit named new instances, and accidental duplicate WezTerm windows.

directories.purpose: Contract-only maps, registries, schemas, workflows, composition graph, and public synthetic fixtures.
Opt-in local validation hooks; never installed implicitly.
Scoped agent procedures and triggerable operating judgment.
Dependency-free cross-platform fixture and registration contracts.
English operator reports, current gaps, and proof ceilings.
Windows and Linux exact-head contract proof.

commands.name: Dependency-free contracts
commands.proof: registry, workflow, fixture, graph, and central-manifest semantics

knownTraps: A second window attached to the same tmux session is not an independent workspace.
Raw WezTerm process count does not prove window identity, activation, or duplication.
Default open-or-activate must not silently behave like explicit new-instance.
Explicit new-instance requires a stable instance ID, a distinct frontend process, and a unique tmux session.
One operator request producing more than one top-level window is duplicate-detected, not success.
Static configuration and CI do not prove live window behavior or operator acceptance.
proofCeiling: Tracked launch-mode harness structure, deterministic fixture semantics, dedicated composition topology, and offline central-manifest registration only.
